parameters-en.Time A: Adjusts the length of time before the first repeat of delay line A. When set to note values, Time follows the system tempo.
parameters-en.Time B: Adjusts the length of time before the first repeat of delay line B. When set to note values, Time follows the system tempo.
parameters-en.Feedbk A: Controls the number of repeats for delay line A. When set to 0%, only one repeat is heard.
parameters-en.Feedbk B: Controls the number of repeats for delay line B. When set to 0%, only one repeat is heard.
parameters-en.Mix: Controls the blend between the delay repeats and the dry signal. At 0%, no repeats are heard; at 100%, no dry signal is heard.
parameters-en.Level: Sets the overall level of the block.
parameters-en.Crossfeed: Controls the amount of the A delay line fed back into the B delay line and vice versa.
parameters-en.Headroom: Some delay pedals' internal signal paths exhibit a bit of grit, especially when placed after a high-gain amp block. Negative values increase the perceived amount of grit; positive values clean things up a bit.
parameters-en.Mod Rate: Controls the rate or speed of modulation.
parameters-en.ModDepth: Controls the depth or amount of modulation.
parameters-en.Shape: Selects the modulation's wave shape (Sine or Triangle).
parameters-en.Phase: Determines the modulation's phase relationship between the two delay lines. At 0, the delay lines modulate together; at 180, modulation is inverted from one another.
parameters-en.Bit Depth: Lowers the bit depth of the delay repeats for a grungier sound. For more transparent results, set to 24 bits.
parameters-en.Sample Rate: Lowers the sample rate of the delay repeats for a grungier, more vintage digital sound. For more transparent results, set to 48kHz.
parameters-en.Low Cut: Applies a low cut (high pass) filter to the repeats, letting you remove the effected signal below a certain frequency.
parameters-en.High Cut: Applies a high cut (low pass) filter to the repeats, letting you remove the effected signal above a certain frequency.
